mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5% fall below 0.5% on average, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 107.5 signals robust manufacturing activity that supports corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO > 107.5 periods fall below 0.8% on average, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns fails to explain more than 10% of variance in out-of-sample tests, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS above 2% and persistently high CPIAUCSL (>240) leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to increased equity discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in tightening regimes exceed -1.2% in more than 40% of cases, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 140,000 thousand combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signals strong labor market dynamics that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to predict SPX returns with a Rank IC > 0.15 in out-of-sample tests, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) below 440,000 million combined with rising housing starts (HOUST > 1,200) indicates shifting demand patterns that create positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-HOUST interaction fails to explain more than 8% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the signal generates negative returns in over 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO < 106.5) during a period of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 240) signals stagflation risks that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in stagflation regimes exceed -2.5% in more than 30% of cases, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03).
